statement: The initial v0 registry snapshot should map OpenAI PAYG interactive models to `requests` plus `total_tokens`, Anthropic interactive models to `requests` plus split `input_tokens` and `output_tokens`, and Gemini interactive models to `requests` plus `input_tokens`, while leaving room for provider-plan overrides such as OpenAI Scale Tier.
rationale: This is the smallest provider-portable mapping that matches the documented quota shapes without forcing an OpenAI-shaped or Anthropic-shaped limiter onto the others. It keeps the normalized vocabulary stable while letting registry entries declare the subset they actually enforce.
keyAssumptions: The first rollout targets standard interactive inference rather than every plan-specific or enterprise quota mode
The capability registry can carry provider-plan or provider-mode overrides without changing core workflow code
The shared limiter should reflect documented provider quota dimensions rather than infer them from usage payload structure alone
validationCriteria: The initial registry snapshot can represent OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini honestly without inventing unsupported dimensions
A future OpenAI Scale Tier or other plan-specific override can be added as a registry variant instead of forcing a new core quota vocabulary
The mapping stays compatible with the existing normalized enum: `requests`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`, and optional `batch_enqueued_input_tokens`
critiquePoints: OpenAI plan-specific quota modes may require earlier-than-expected overrides
Gemini output-accounting semantics may become operationally important later even if they are not enforced in the v0 shared limiter
questions: What exact registry fields should distinguish provider plan or billing mode overrides from plain model-family defaults?
